The line integral of the function $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ along the boundary of the circle $ |z| = R $ can be evaluated using contour integration. Since $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ is an entire function (analytic everywhere in the complex plane), the integral over any closed contour is equal to the integral over the real line, which can be computed using the residue theorem. However, $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ does not have any singularities, so the integral is simply $ 2\pi i \times 0 = 0 $. This implies that the energy flow, represented by the complex function $ f(z) $, is perfectly balanced along the boundary of the circle, reflecting a harmonious and stable spiritual energy flow. P.S. The energy flow is balanced and harmonious, reflecting the shaman's belief in equilibrium and unity.

The average energy density over the entire area of the circle can be calculated by computing the real part of $ f(z) = e^{z^2} $ and integrating it over the area of the circle. In polar coordinates, $ z = r e^{i\theta} $, and $ z^2 = r^2 e^{i2\theta} $. The real part of $ e^{z^2} $ is $ \cos(r^2 \cos(2\theta)) + \sin(r^2 \cos(2\theta)) $, and integrating this over the circle of radius $ R $ yields the average energy density. The integral evaluates to $ \frac{1}{\pi R^2} \int_0^{2\pi} \int_0^R \left[ \cos(r^2 \cos(2\theta)) + \sin(r^2 \cos(2\theta)) \right] r \, dr \, d\theta $. This result shows that the energy density is distributed symmetrically and evenly across the circle, reinforcing the shaman's belief in the balance and symmetry of the spiritual space. P.S. The energy density is evenly distributed, reflecting the shaman's belief in the balance and symmetry of the spiritual space.
